assertions.reason: The Environment label control still only exposes preset staging/qa/production options, so the empty-value rejection path is not reachable through visible controls.
The create/detail study surfaces still do not expose the persisted createdBy identity on the real user surface.
Draft studies still visibly expose a Launch Study button and launch dialog.
A study created against a draft persona pack reached the launch confirmation dialog, but confirming launch returned “A published persona pack is required before launch.”
The production launch dialog warns that acknowledgement is required, but it only exposes Cancel and Confirm Launch, so there is no visible no-ack submission path to validate rejection.
Entering activeConcurrency=40 through the visible control saved the study summary as 30, so the runtime-bypass path required by the assertion remains unreachable through the browser.
After archiving the linked pack, confirming launch returned “A published persona pack is required before launch.”
The study wizard still offers only selector-based pack choices and no visible way to submit an arbitrary non-existent personaPackId.

frictions: The Active concurrency field accepted 40 in the wizard, but the saved study summary clamped the visible value back to 30.
blockers: The Environment label and persona-pack inputs are still selector-driven, so empty environment and arbitrary non-existent-pack submission paths remain unreachable through visible controls.
The current study detail surface still does not expose creator identity metadata.
